message:
fix(views): AdjacencyView.copy for MultiGraph compatibility (br-adjcp)

Earlier br-atlascp added copy() to AtlasView. MultiGraph's inner
adjacency uses AdjacencyView (G.adj[u] -> dict-of-keyed-attrs); it
also lacked copy(), so nx.to_dict_of_dicts(fnx_multigraph) crashed
with AttributeError: 'AdjacencyView' object has no attribute 'copy'.

Added copy() to AdjacencyView (returns dict-of-dicts snapshot) and
to MultiAdjacencyView (returns dict-of-dicts-of-dicts recursively).

3 regression tests in TestAdjacencyViewCopy:
- nx.to_dict_of_dicts end-to-end on fnx MultiGraph
- AdjacencyView.copy() direct
- MultiAdjacencyView.copy() deep snapshot

Test suite: 4145 -> 4149 passed, 0 regressions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -170,7 +170,7 @@
 {"id":"franken_networkx-69m","title":"Graph Products: corona, modular, rooted, lexicographic products","description":"Graph Products

Implementations of standard graph product operations that combine two graphs into a new graph.

## Functions to Implement

- `tensor_product(G, H) -> Graph`: Nodes are (g,h) pairs. Edge ((g1,h1),(g2,h2)) iff (g1,g2) in G.edges AND (h1,h2) in H.edges. Also called categorical/kronecker product.
- `cartesian_product(G, H) -> Graph`: Edge ((g1,h1),(g2,h2)) iff (g1==g2 AND (h1,h2) in H.edges) OR ((g1,g2) in G.edges AND h1==h2).
- `lexicographic_product(G, H) -> Graph`: Edge ((g1,h1),(g2,h2)) iff (g1,g2) in G.edges OR (g1==g2 AND (h1,h2) in H.edges).
- `strong_product(G, H) -> Graph`: Union of tensor and cartesian products.
- `corona_product(G, H) -> Graph`: Take G plus |G| copies of H, connect each node g_i to all nodes in H_i.
- `rooted_product(G, H, root) -> Graph`: Take |G| copies of H, identify root node of each H copy with corresponding G node.
- `power(G, k) -> Graph`: G^k where nodes are adjacent if distance ≤ k in G.

## Algorithmic Approach

- Product node space: iterate over itertools.product(G.nodes, H.nodes) for all products.
- Tensor: iterate all edge pairs from G and H.
- Cartesian: for each G edge, connect across all H nodes; for each H edge, connect across all G nodes.
- Power: BFS from each node up to depth k, add edges to all discovered nodes.
- Node attributes: merge from both factor graphs. Edge attributes: merge where applicable.

## Performance Targets

- Products of graphs with 1K nodes each (→ 1M product nodes): < 10 seconds.
- Power graph: O(n * (n + m)) using BFS from each node.

## Edge Cases

- Empty graph factors → empty product.
- Single-node factors.
- Self-loops in factor graphs.
- Directed vs undirected factor graphs (must match or raise error).
- Multigraph factors.
- Node attribute conflicts between G and H.
- power(G, 0) → no edges; power(G, 1) → G itself.
- corona_product with H being empty graph.

## Test Requirements

- Unit tests: verify node count = |G| * |H| for standard products, edge counts match formulas.
- Algebraic identity tests:
  - cartesian_product(G, H) isomorphic to cartesian_product(H, G) (commutativity).
  - tensor_product(G, H) isomorphic to tensor_product(H, G).
  - strong_product = union of tensor and cartesian edge sets.
- NX cross-validation: compare edge sets with NetworkX on small graphs (K3, P4, C5, Petersen).
- Property tests: product of connected graphs is connected for cartesian product.
- Attribute preservation tests: node/edge attributes from factor graphs survive in product.
- Known graph tests: cartesian_product(P2, P3) = grid_graph([2,3]).
- power(cycle(n), k) edge count verification.
- Round-trip tests: verify product graph properties (e.g., tensor product of bipartite graphs is bipartite).
- Stress tests: products of moderate-size graphs to verify performance targets.

## Implementation Notes

- Pure Python implementation; graph construction dominates runtime.
- Depends on core graph types, itertools, BFS (for power).
- Must preserve directed/undirected type from input graphs.
- Node naming convention: tuple (g, h) for product nodes.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-22T02:19:47.612874210Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-22T03:36:21.318196593Z","closed_at":"2026-03-22T03:36:21.317579246Z","close_reason":"Implemented 4 graph products: corona_product, modular_product, rooted_product, lexicographic_product. Total: 488 functions.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["algorithms","tier-3"]}

 {"id": "franken_networkx-adjkset", "title": "REVIEW: [HIGH] AdjacencyView.keys() returns plain iterator — breaks nx.non_neighbors set arithmetic", "description": "nx.non_neighbors does `graph._adj.keys() - graph._adj[node].keys() - {node}` expecting dict_keys-like views with set-operator support. fnx's _adjacency_view_keys returned a plain `iter(self)` generator, so the subtraction raised TypeError: unsupported operand type(s) for -. This broke many nx algorithms that use non_neighbors internally, including approximation.clique_removal and ramsey.ramsey_R2. Fix: _adjacency_view_keys now returns a _AdjKeysView wrapping a collections.abc.KeysView around a list snapshot, so -, |, &, ^ all work.", "status": "closed", "closed_at": "2026-04-24T07:52:51.336735Z", "close_reason": "Fixed: _adjacency_view_keys returns a KeysView subclass with full set-operator support. Applied to AdjacencyView, MultiAdjacencyView, AtlasView, _GRAPH_ADJACENCY_VIEW_TYPE, _DIGRAPH_ADJACENCY_VIEW_TYPE, and EdgeView types.", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "bug", "created_at": "2026-04-24T07:52:51.336735Z", "created_by": "cc-networkx", "updated_at": "2026-04-24T07:52:51.336735Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}
 {"id": "franken_networkx-grattrset", "title": "REVIEW: [HIGH] G.graph = {...} assignment raises AttributeError — breaks nx.subgraph_view", "description": "nx internals (subgraph_view, restricted_view, relabel) do `newG.graph = G.graph` but fnx Graph.graph is a read-only getset_descriptor. _GraphAttrsDescriptor adds a __set__ that clears+updates the existing dict in place.", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "bug", "created_at": "2026-04-24T07:59:34.819022Z", "created_by": "cc-networkx", "updated_at": "2026-04-24T07:59:34.819022Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "closed_at": "2026-04-24T07:59:34.819022Z", "close_reason": "Fixed in cycle-8."}
 {"id": "franken_networkx-adjprivset", "title": "REVIEW: [HIGH] _node/_adj assignment AttributeError blocks nx.subgraph_view pipeline", "description": "After br-grattrset fixed the .graph setter, nx.subgraph_view then tries `newG._node = FilterAtlas(...)`. fnx._node is a read-only property. Making it settable alone is insufficient because fnx.Graph.__iter__ reads the Rust storage directly, bypassing any user-set override — so FilterAtlas-based filtering would not work. Proper fix requires Rust-side Graph to support external adjacency/node storage. Queued.", "status": "open", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "bug", "created_at": "2026-04-24T07:59:34.819022Z", "created_by": "cc-networkx", "updated_at": "2026-04-24T07:59:34.819022Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}
+{"id": "franken_networkx-adjcp", "title": "REVIEW: [HIGH] AdjacencyView lacks copy() — nx.to_dict_of_dicts breaks on fnx MultiGraph", "description": "Earlier br-atlascp added copy() to AtlasView. MultiGraph's inner adjacency uses AdjacencyView (G.adj[u] -> dict-of-keyed-attrs); it also lacked copy(), so nx.to_dict_of_dicts(fnx_multigraph) crashed with AttributeError: 'AdjacencyView' object has no attribute 'copy'. Added copy() to AdjacencyView (returns dict-of-dicts snapshot) and to MultiAdjacencyView (returns dict-of-dicts-of-dicts recursively).", "status": "closed", "closed_at": "2026-04-24T08:08:51.243295Z", "close_reason": "Fixed: AdjacencyView.copy() and MultiAdjacencyView.copy() return plain dict-snapshot structures. 3 regression tests cover nx.to_dict_of_dicts on fnx MultiGraph + direct copy() calls.", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "bug", "created_at": "2026-04-24T08:08:51.243295Z", "created_by": "cc-networkx", "updated_at": "2026-04-24T08:08:51.243295Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}

@@ -1144,3 +1144,44 @@ def test_all_four_classes_support_graph_assign(self):
             G = cls()
             G.graph = {"role": "test"}
             assert dict(G.graph) == {"role": "test"}
+
+
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# Regression: franken_networkx-adjcp — AdjacencyView.copy for MultiGraph
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+
+class TestAdjacencyViewCopy:
+    """Earlier br-atlascp added copy() to AtlasView. MultiGraph's inner
+    adjacency is an AdjacencyView (G.adj[u] -> dict-of-keyed-attrs); it
+    also lacked copy(), so nx.to_dict_of_dicts(fnx_multigraph) crashed
+    with 'AdjacencyView object has no attribute copy'.
+    """
+
+    def test_adj_view_copy_on_multigraph(self):
+        import networkx as nx
+
+        MG = fnx.MultiGraph()
+        MG.add_edge(0, 1, key="a", w=1)
+        MG.add_edge(0, 1, key="b", w=2)
+        MG.add_edge(1, 2, key=0, w=3)
+        MGn = nx.MultiGraph()
+        MGn.add_edge(0, 1, key="a", w=1)
+        MGn.add_edge(0, 1, key="b", w=2)
+        MGn.add_edge(1, 2, key=0, w=3)
+        assert nx.to_dict_of_dicts(MG) == nx.to_dict_of_dicts(MGn)
+
+    def test_adj_view_copy_directly(self):
+        MG = fnx.MultiGraph()
+        MG.add_edge(0, 1, key="a")
+        MG.add_edge(0, 1, key="b")
+        assert MG.adj[0].copy() == {1: {"a": {}, "b": {}}}
+
+    def test_multi_adj_view_copy_deep(self):
+        """MultiAdjacencyView.copy() should snapshot the full
+        dict-of-dicts-of-dicts structure.
+        """
+        MG = fnx.MultiGraph()
+        MG.add_edge(0, 1, key=0, w=5)
+        snapshot = MG.adj.copy()
+        assert snapshot == {0: {1: {0: {"w": 5}}}, 1: {0: {0: {"w": 5}}}}
